plugins.name: Git status
plugins.description: An inline panel that follows the active session — the branch, uncommitted changes and unpushed/behind commits of the repo it is working in, refreshing when the session switches or runs a git command — plus a sortable dialog for the same across all your configured repositories. Click to drop a status summary into the session. Manage the repository list in settings.
plugins.author: Cockpit

versions.notes: Added an inline 'Session repo' panel that follows the active session — the git status of the repo it is working in, refreshing when you switch sessions or the session runs a git command. Needs a cockpit build with the session read/observe surface.
Initial release — per-repo branch / uncommitted / unpushed / ahead-behind via 'git status --porcelain --branch', with inject/copy of a summary.

plugins.name: Prompt Library
plugins.description: A library of reusable prompt templates in the left menu. Click one to insert it into the active session, filling in any {{variable}} fields first. Create, edit and remove templates in the same dialog. Plus a quick-insert palette (the Ctrl+Shift+P shortcut, or the command palette): search your prompts and drop one into the session with a click or Enter.
plugins.author: Cockpit

versions.notes: Clearer selected-item highlight in the quick-insert list.
Fixed the quick-insert arrow keys not moving the selection.
Fixed the quick-insert search-bar icon rendering as a green emoji on Windows — now a monochrome accent chevron.
The quick-insert is now a spotlight-style search bar (rounded pill + accent icon) instead of a plain box.
Removed the redundant 'Insert prompt' menu button — the quick-insert stays on Ctrl+Shift+P and in the command palette.

Clearer selected-template highlight, plus a quick-insert palette (Insert prompt button / Ctrl+Shift+P) to search and drop a prompt into the session. Needs a cockpit build with the plugin shortcut surface.
Initial release - reusable prompt templates with {{variable}} substitution, click-to-insert into the active session, in-dialog management, five starter templates.

plugins.name: GitHub Issues
plugins.description: Browse open GitHub issues across your repos (via the gh CLI) or one repo in a searchable, sortable dialog with a repository filter; click one to drop a prompt asking the agent to open and review it. The prompt template is editable in settings.
plugins.author: Cockpit

versions.notes: Added an "Assigned to me" filter (gh --assignee @me across repos, or the REST assignee filter for a single repo).
gh CLI + cross-repo, searchable/sortable dialog with a repository filter (All + per-repo), per-field settings help tooltips.
Initial release.

plugins.name: GitHub Pull Requests
plugins.description: Shows your open GitHub pull requests inline under the session list (how many is configurable, and you can limit it to specific repositories), refreshing both on a timer and the instant a session opens/merges/closes a PR (it watches session output for a pull url or a merged/closed line), via the gh CLI across your repos or one repo, plus a dialog listing every open PR in a searchable, sortable grid; left-click a PR to drop a review prompt, or right-click for a menu (add to prompt / open in browser). The prompt template is editable in settings.
plugins.author: Cockpit

versions.notes: The inline PR count is now configurable (1-20), and you can limit the list to specific repositories (owner/repo per line; blank = all).
Added a right-click context menu to the inline PR rows: 'Add to prompt' (the normal left-click action) and 'Open in browser'.

Smart refresh — the inline section now also refreshes the instant a session opens, merges or closes a PR, by watching session output for a pull url or a merged/closed line (on top of the 60s timer). Needs a cockpit build with the session read/observe surface.
Added: the inline PR section auto-refreshes (60s) so PRs appear/disappear on their own as they are opened, merged or closed.
Fixed: showed no PRs for the default @me owner — now searches --author @me (your PRs across all repos, including org repos) instead of --owner @me (only repos you own).
Added an "Assigned to me" filter (gh --assignee @me across repos, or a client-side assignees filter for a single repo).
Inline Open PRs section + all-PRs dialog, gh CLI or single-repo HTTP. Hardened JSON parsing; per-field settings help tooltips; settings apply live (no restart).

plugins.name: YouTrack
plugins.description: Connect one or more YouTrack instances and browse their open issues per project over the REST API (permanent token); a dialog with instance/project/state filters lets you narrow down, and clicking an issue drops a prompt asking the agent to work on it. The prompt template is editable in settings.
plugins.author: Cockpit

versions.notes: Added a Shift+Y keyboard shortcut that opens the issues dialog (via the new plugin shortcut SDK). Needs a cockpit build with the keyboard-shortcuts system.
Added an "Assigned to me" filter (YouTrack's "for: me" clause added to the query).

Registers the JetBrains remote YouTrack MCP server (/mcp, per configured instance) so a session can query YouTrack via tools. Requires YouTrack Cloud 2026.2+ with the MCP server enabled + a permanent token.
Multiple YouTrack instances; left-menu button opening a dialog with instance/project/state filters (All). Per-field settings help tooltips; no personal instance baked in.
Single-instance per-project open issues over the YouTrack REST API.

plugins.name: Gemini / OpenAI Provider
plugins.description: Adds Gemini and OpenAI as selectable session providers, both over an OpenAI-compatible chat-completions endpoint (Gemini's own OpenAI-compat endpoint, or api.openai.com directly). Configure an API key and model per profile in Manage profiles. Experimental (0.x) — the provider-plugin mechanism is new; live-verify with a real key.
plugins.author: Cockpit

versions.notes: Experimental first provider-plugin (#45 phase A): Gemini + OpenAI via the OpenAI-compatible endpoint over Microsoft.Extensions.AI. Trimmed capability flags; API key redacted from ToString; graceful handling when the provider plugin is missing.

plugins.name: CLI Agent Provider (Codex)
plugins.description: Adds Codex CLI as a selectable session provider, driven as a subprocess per turn (codex exec --json, resumed for follow-up turns). Requires the codex CLI installed and authenticated on the machine running Cockpit. Experimental (0.x, phase B1) — event mapping is a best-effort reconstruction; verify against your Codex version.
plugins.author: Cockpit

versions.notes: Phase B1: Codex CLI subprocess provider through the plugin session-driver contract (process-per-turn, resume, stderr-drain, interrupt=kill). Live Codex schema/auth/resume verification pending (B2).

plugins.name: GitHub Models
plugins.description: Adds GitHub Models as a selectable session provider over its OpenAI-compatible endpoint (models.github.ai/inference). Configure a GitHub personal access token (models:read scope) and a namespaced model id (e.g. openai/gpt-4.1) per profile. This is GitHub's official models API — a Copilot subscription raises the rate limits.
plugins.author: Cockpit

versions.notes: GitHub Models via the OpenAI-compatible endpoint over Microsoft.Extensions.AI (same driver as the Gemini/OpenAI provider). Needs a GitHub PAT with models:read.
